items.title: World loses patience with Trump as war becomes about more than Iran

children.content: Global leaders are giving up the pretence of diplomatic niceties as they lose patience with the US president's actions.

image.altText: Donald Trump speaks into the ear of Emmanual Macron, both of which are posing for a photo against a white backdrop

items.title: The 20,000 sailors who have become sitting ducks in the Gulf

children.content: Interviews and first-hand accounts from sailors stranded in the Persian Gulf — under threat of drone attacks, missiles, and exploding boats — reveal an increasingly dire humanitarian crisis unfolding.

image.altText: A satellite photo of a lot of large ships floating in the sea.

items.title: How the Iran war is pushing US ally the Philippines into economic crisis

children.content: Countries heavily reliant on imported oil are particularly vulnerable to the unfolding energy crisis caused by the US-Israel war with Iran. It's no more apparent than in South-East Asian countries like the Philippines.

image.altText: An older man in a white shirt leans against a bus.

items.title: Cops would not have had 'luxury' of taking Dezi Freeman alive, experts say

children.content: A former police hostage negotiator says he was sure Dezi Freeman's seven-month run from the law "was going to end in tears" and that the fugitive would never surrender.

image.altText: A black police armourned vehicle is parked next to a shipping container. A black pipe is connected between the two.

items.title: Strategically critical Pacific island divided over growing US military presence

children.content: The Pacific Island of Guam sits at the centre of a struggle between the world's most powerful military and the right of its people to determine their own future.

gallery.altText: A drone shot of Guam's coast line.
A sign on a fence warning people not to enter.
A handful of men in military uniform behind a truck.

thumbnail.altText: A drone shot of Guam's coast line.
A sign on a fence warning people not to enter.
A handful of men in military uniform behind a truck.

items.title: Your pocket guide to daylight savings ending in Australia

image.altText: A woman reaches out to an alarm clock from bed.

children.content: The air is getting cooler and the days are about to get shorter as daylight savings comes to an end this weekend.

items.title: Considering downgrading your private health cover? These are the questions to ask

children.content: The loopholes to look for if you're thinking about downgrading or changing your private health insurance.

image.altText: A graphic which has health care cards on it.

items.title: I find myself weeping again for Lebanon

children.content: Lebanese families have been told to flee homes that they might never be able to return to, and families are setting up tents for an uncertain and dangerous life on the streets of Beirut all because of a conflict not of their making, brought about by another state.

image.altText: Women walk by a damaged building in Beirut.

avatar.alt: Virginia Trioli in a stylish suit on a set with lighting behind her

items.title: How nightmare Severe Tropical Cyclone Narelle became a historic storm

children.content: From the very first news reports about Severe Tropical Cyclone Narelle, it was clear this wasn't just another storm. This is the story of how the cyclone impacted the entire span of northern Australia and how communities came together to pick up the pieces.

image.altText: Timelapse of Tropical Cyclone Narelle's path

items.title: 'So few precedents': One Nation leaves SA Liberals clinging to opposition status

children.content: The Liberal Party will have just one more seat than One Nation in South Australia’s lower house, leaving opposition leader Ashton Hurn little margin for error.

image.altText: composite image of Cory Bernardi outdoors and Ashton Hurn in a dark room, both looking serious

items.title: Poo of 'endearing' bat worth hundreds of millions, study says

children.content: Bats are often thought of as smelly, ugly and noisy, but a study has revealed the economic and ecological value of one Australian species's poo.

image.altText: A grey-headed flying fox hanging from an aviary roof eating from a pink plant as it hangs upside down.

items.title: Voss unfazed about mounting speculation over his future as Blues coach

children.content: Michael Voss knows he faces more scrutiny over his role as Blues coach after his side endures another second-half meltdown.

children.content: Melbourne Storm coach Craig Bellamy puts his players on notice after they were thrashed 50-10 by Penrith on Good Friday.

image.altText: Cameron Munster and Storm teammates re-group during their NRL loss to the Panthers.

items.title: 10 podcasts we can't stop listening to at the moment

children.content: The podcasts we're listening to this weekend include an investigation into the Salt Path scandal and a personal look at the role of Australia in the end of apartheid.

items.title: Trump's fiercest female defenders are now on the outer

items.title: Judge dismisses Lively's sexual harassment claims against Baldoni

items.title: What to know about cafes using surcharges to offset fuel crisis impact

items.title: Dockers claim thriller after Crow hits post inside final minute

children.content: Fremantle coughs up a five-goal lead before holding on in the final quarter after Adelaide's James Borlase hits the post with 30 seconds left on the clock.

items.title: Myanmar junta chief Min Aung Hlaing elected president

children.content: In a live broadcast of the vote count the former commander-in-chief comfortably passed the threshold required to win the presidency.

items.title: Wet noses and silky coats helping to steady nerves ahead of court dates

children.content: Therapy dogs in courthouses are helping victims and witnesses of crimes to confront some of their darkest moments, but volunteers say many more are needed.

items.title: FIFA raises top ticket price for World Cup final to nearly $16,000

children.content: The top ticket price was $15,900, a sharp increase from the original $12,560 when FIFA sold tickets after the tournament draw in December.

items.title: Woods heard telling police he was 'just talking to the president' after crash

children.content: Police have released body camera footage showing Tiger Woods's arrest after he crashed his SUV last week in Florida.

image.altText: A still image from the bodycam footage of Tiger Woods speaking to police after his car crash in Florida.

items.title: A 'rare parasitic fungus' is infecting Top End footballers. Here's what we know

children.content: Health authorities say a rare water-borne pathogen is behind the recent outbreak of severe conjunctivitis in Top End football clubs, with a number of Darwin playing fields deemed high-risk infection sites.

image.altText: a man taking a mark in a football game, splashing in mud.

items.title: As more Australian businesses expand into Asia, here's some cultural differences to consider

items.title: From Hobart to the world stage: Theatre community mourns death of a giant

children.content: A giant of Australian theatre who helped launch the careers of some of the country's biggest names of the stage has died aged 93.

items.title: Dirt bike rider, 14, among three dead in separate WA Easter long weekend crashes

children.content: The long weekend is off to a tragic start after two motorcyclists die in separate crashes near Perth, while a woman, 18, is killed in a four-wheel drive rollover in WA's north.

image.altText: A door of a police car with police tape tied to the side mirror.

items.title: Hospitality operators call for end to Rottnest's staffing advantage

children.content: Visa rules allow backpackers to conduct "remote work" on the island, just 33km from Perth's city centre, but not in WA's South West — something business owners want to change.

image.altText: Two girls at a coffee shop

items.title: SA Electoral Commission apologises to staff for post-election pay delay

children.content: The Electoral Commission of South Australia has apologised to staff who have not been fully paid almost a fortnight after the state election, saying a payroll processing delay had caused the problem.

image.altText: An electoral officer at a polling station.

items.title: Sick of not knowing if your avocado is ripe or bruised? This scanner could change that

children.content: An Australian-first avocado "ripeness scanner" is being trialled, with the aim of telling customers exactly when their avocados will be ready to eat.

gallery.altText: A close up of green avocados inside a white crate.

thumbnail.altText: A close up of green avocados inside a white crate.
